reasoning: The informal statement says: if a group has exactly one element of order 2, that element is in the center. The Lean signature encodes "exactly one element of order 2" via the combination of `hx : orderOf x = 2` (existence — x is such an element) and `hx1 : ∀ y, orderOf y = 2 → y = x` (uniqueness — any element of order 2 equals x). Together these are equivalent to "x is the unique element of order 2". The conclusion `x ∈ center G` matches "that element is in the center of the group". The group G is an arbitrary group (universally quantified as `{G : Type*} [Group G]`), matching the informal universal reading. No hypotheses are silently added or dropped.
